name: Le Montrachet Lobster Bisque
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds lobster tails, chopped into large chunks
salt and white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a 6-quart saucepan until it begins to smoke.
2. Add lobster pieces. Stir until lobster turns red.
3. Separate shells from meat and return shells to the pot.
4. Chop meat into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
5. Add carrots, onions, and celery to shells and stir until onion is tender.
6. Add white wine and simmer until reduced by half.
7. Add brandy. Bring mixture to a simmer and carefully ignite, keeping face and clothing away from flames.
8. When flames die down, add water, garlic, tomato paste, paprika, cayenne pepper, thyme, tarragon, bay leaves, and black pepper. Stir well.
9. Bring to boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 35 minutes, stirring occasionally.
10. Strain through a cheesecloth into a clean 2-quart saucepan.
11. Soften butter in a small bowl and knead in flour. Mix until the flour-butter mixture is dissolved and the soup has a uniform thick texture, about 5 minutes.
12. Add hot cream. Season with salt and white pepper.
13. Simmer for 1 minute.
14. Add sherry and stir. Remove from heat.
